Get In Touch > Email Local Elections 2026 I am Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP and I'm proud to be the Member of Parliament for Balham, Earlsfield, Furzedown, Tooting and Wandsworth Common. Since being elected in 2016, I have helped tens of thousands of local residents. If you need my help, or would like to ask me a question, please get in touch. pages.title: About Me - Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP Home About Me News Subscribe to my Newsletter How can I help? Get In Touch > Email Local Elections 2026 About me My name is Dr Rosena Allin-Khan. I was born and raised in Tooting and I have been proud to represent our community as Labour MP for Balham, Earlsfield, Furzedown, Tooting and Wandsworth Common since 2016. I grew up locally in Tooting, attending Trinity St Mary's School in Balham, and I’m very happily raising my family here. Before getting into politics, I worked full-time as a Junior Doctor at St George's Hospital and I still do shifts there in the A&E department. My medical career also took me overseas as a humanitarian doctor, where I have supported efforts in some of the biggest humanitarian crises in the Middle East, Africa, and Asia. Most notably, I have worked in the Rohingya refugee camps on the border of Myanmar to support refugees fleeing an unfolding genocide and in Palestine to highlight the need for better access to medical treatment for Palestinians. More recently, at the beginning of the war in Ukraine in early 2022, I travelled to Lviv to provide humanitarian supplies and support to civilians who were injured during Russia's invasion as well as offer emergency triage training to Ukrainian medics. Closer to home, my political journey began when I was first elected as a Wandsworth Labour Councillor for Tooting Bec and Balham in 2014 , where I proudly served as the Deputy Leader of the Labour Group. That first election over ten years ago now really put the fire in my stomach for campaigning and advocating for local people. Following Sadiq Khan stepping down to run for Mayor of London, I was asked by many in the community to stand to be the next Member of Parliament for Tooting. I ran and won in the by-election held in June 2016 , which tragically coincided with the murder of Labour MP Jo Cox to whom I paid tribute on election night in my acceptance speech. ​During my time as the local MP, I have campaigned to tackle knife crime in our community, protect local community assets such as the Tooting Bec Athletics Club and Fishponds Playing Fields, and for action to be taken to tackle the climate crisis. I am incredibly proud to have founded the Safe Space scheme locally , in which local businesses sign up to be a place of sanctuary for those feeling unsafe day or night. Look out for the green sticker on the windows of local shops, restaurants, and pubs! Additionally, I have helped secure step-free access at Wandsworth Common railway station, and I'm currently running a campaign for a lift to be installed at Tooting Broadway tube in order to allow hospital patients, wheelchair users, parents with buggies, and many others access to the Underground network. I've also overseen the opening of many new parks and green spaces in the local area, including the Mellison Road pocket park in Tooting, the Swaffield Road pocket park in Earlsfield, and Springfield Park on the redeveloped hospital site. The latter park was the largest new green space open in London in the last decade and I was proud to open it alongside the Mayor of London and Leader of Wandsworth Council. Nationally, I served as Labour's Shadow Cabinet Minister for Mental Health until 2023, calling out Government failures and developing policies. I put together a bold vision for transforming mental health services , including pledges to recruit 8,500 additional mental health staff, place a mental health specialist in every school and an open access hub in every community for young people. More broadly, I've been vocal on many issues near and dear to our local community here in Tooting. Having worked in the West Bank and Palestinian hospitals in Lebanon, the cause of Palestinian statehood is incredibly close to my heart. I have continuously advocated for the Palestinian people throughout my tenure as an MP , particularly over the last two years as Gaza has been torn apart. Other key priorities

pages.title: Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP - News
pages.text_excerpt: Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP - News Dr Rosena Allin-Khan MP Home About Me News Subscribe to my Newsletter How can I help? Get In Touch > Email Local Elections 2026 News VAWG Roundtable 26/3/2026 Tackling the scourge of Violence Against Women and Girls requires a united front. Yesterday, I was proud to bring together fantastic local organisations from across Tooting for a vital roundtable discussion on this very issue. Real, lasting change cannot be imposed from the top down. It must be built from the ground up, in partnership with those who understand the challenges best. Listening to the expertise of these local groups, it is clear that co-creating innovative solutions within our community is the only way forward. Their insights are indispensable in shaping strategies that will truly work for local people. Happy City Index 23/3/2026 Last Friday in Parliament, I had the great pleasure of hosting the unveiling of the Happy City Index 2026. It was an incredibly proud moment to see London officially recognised with Gold City status, placing it among the top 50 happiest cities on earth. This wonderful achievement is a testament to the vibrancy, diversity, and spirit that defines our capital. From the bustling streets of my own community in Tooting to the cultural hubs of the West End, our city is a place of endless opportunity and human connection. For too long, we've heard voices talking London down, but this recognition shows that narrative for the nonsense it is. This celebrates the millions of people who make our capital the dynamic, welcoming, and brilliantly unique place that we all love. It's a powerful reminder of why we must always champion our great city. Hatzola Attack 23/3/2026 The antisemitic attack on the Hatzola team in Golders Green is utterly shameful. These volunteer medics dedicate their time to saving lives, serving their community with unwavering commitment. To see their ambulances targeted is simply sickening. Having worked alongside the Hatzola team for many years during my time as a doctor, I have seen first-hand the dedication and hard work they put in. Their selfless professionalism is something to be admired. An assault on those who run towards danger to help others is disgraceful, but for it to be driven by antisemitic hate makes it even more abhorrent. Such vile incidents cause deep fear and cannot be tolerated. Full solidarity must be shown to the Hatzola medics and the entire Jewish community at this time. We must stand united against this hatred. Saturday Evening Shift 22/3/2026 ​Another Saturday evening shift in A&E last night was a powerful reminder of the pressures our health service is facing. The relentless pace and the sheer dedication of my colleagues never fails to humble me, as they work tirelessly to care for our community under incredible strain. Amidst the rough and tumble of politics, these shifts keep me grounded. They ensure the challenges facing our NHS are never just abstract debates for me, but real-life struggles for patients and staff. Serving my community here in Tooting, both on the green benches in Westminster and in the emergency department, is an immense privilege. My experiences on the ward directly inform my work as an MP, strengthening my resolve to fight for a health service with the resources it needs to look after us all. Eid Mubarak 20/3/2026 Eid Mubarak to everyone in Tooting and across the country celebrating the end of the holy month of Ramadan. This sacred time of fasting, prayer, and deep reflection now culminates in a joyous celebration of community and togetherness. The spirit of charity and compassion, so central to Ramadan, truly embodies the very best of our values here in Tooting, powerfully demonstrating the importance of looking out for one another.
